L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where archive entries with relative path components are extracted without validation, allowing an attacker to craft a malicious archive that writes files to arbitrary filesystem locations outside the intended extraction directory.

L3.area: The core extraction module's file writing and directory creation logic during archive decompression
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where archive entries with relative path components (such as directory traversal sequences) are not validated before being used to construct file output paths during extraction. An attacker can craft a malicious archive containing path traversal entries or symbolic link entries that escape the intended output directory, enabling arbitrary file writes on the target system.

ground_truth.reason: The decompress package fails to validate extracted file paths against the output directory, allowing archive entries containing '../' sequences to write files outside the intended extraction directory.
